publications.title: Broad TCR repertoire and diverse structural solutions for recognition of an immunodominant CD8+ T cell epitope.
publications.authors: Song I, Gil A, Mishra R, Ghersi D, Selin LK, Stern LJ.
publications.executive_summary: This study characterizes the structural basis of CD8+ T cell recognition of the influenza M1 epitope presented by HLA-A2, a dominant target of antiviral immunity in infected individuals. Multiple distinct TCRs recognize the same HLA-A2–M1 complex through diverse structural solutions, with most TCRs converging on a small cleft between the MHC molecule and peptide as the primary contact interface. The broad TCR repertoire targeting this immunodominant epitope provides robustness against clonal loss and viral escape, demonstrating how TCR variation confers plasticity in antigen recognition and contributes to effective control of influenza infection.
